The license code exists of several text lines without any special characters.
Example lines of license
Identifier
Details
C AA12345
C
License starts with the license serial number.
Format: 2 letters and 5 or 6 digits.
L 0 -1 -1 -1 -1 20250801 2
L 525 -1 3 10000 10000 -1 -1
L 561 -1
L 535 -1
L 13005 -1
L 37 5
L 30 5
L
License features.
1st number: license / feature number.
2nd number: either -1 (System license) or a single number >0: Station licenses. If there are multiple parameters then it is a Volume license with yearly volume and one-time volume.
Date parameter has format YYYYMMDD (e.g. 20250801) and can be expiration date.
A BB:10:20:30:40:A0
A
MachineID / MAC-ID of (primary) network card of target system.
ID separator needs to be colon.
E 20253007
E
Optional:
Expiration of activation code in format YYYYDDMM.
Afterwards you cannot use this code to activate anymore and need a new one.
X 509899 37AD-199D-1133-AE35 (on old license systems)
or
X 423441 64089626E32685F99EDD40E7D30CFB69EA5
(on newer license systems)
X
Checksum value.
License is only valid, if all license text matches the calculated checksum.

answer:
Where is this setting configured?
In a normal on-premise system, this setting can be adjusted in the Designer under System > System Settings > Database, retention, and reporting > Reporting Server > Data retention period > Documents.
In a multitenant system (Cloud/OPMT), retention settings are set centrally in the Tenant Management System. This is found by clicking into the tenant then scrolling down to the Reporting section, which contains "Document Retention Period".
When is reporting document data retention run?
Reporting document retention is run at the start of an iteration of reporting service processing, once the current time in UTC is greater than the value in TotalAgility_Reporting.retention_stamp.dt_last_retention. Prior to 2026.3, the timestamp is always written without the time component, meaning that retention always triggers at midnight UTC. As of 2026.3, the time reporting retention executes is configurable.
What criteria are used for retention of document data?
Retention is applied to documents that are:
marked completed (TotalAgility_Reporting.doc_dim.is_processing_completed=1), or
deleted from TotalAgility (TotalAgility_Reporting.doc_dim.is_deleted=1)
When is completed/deleted document data removed by retention...
When using KAFTA 1.5+?
KAFTA 1.5s Hourly plan has a step named Update etl_job for processed data which will update TotalAgility_Reporting.etl_job.etl_status to -1 to indicate a range of data has already been loaded into KAFTA. Thus, the data is not needed in reporting anymore, and the data of any completed/deleted documents loaded in KAFTA will be removed in the next run of retention. This happens without regard to the age of the document, or the number of days specified in the retention settings.
It is good for performance that the data is removed once already loaded into KAFTA. But if needed, this behavior can be disabled by disabling the "Update etl_job for processed data" step withing the Hourly Execution Plan. For details see
TAFTA 1.5: Manually Run Execution Plan Cause Missing Data
Without KAFTAs etl_job update?
This applies when using KAFTA 1.4 or if the "Update etl_job for processed data" execution plan step is disabled
When the above KAFTA behavior isnt in effect, reporting retention will remove document data where the document is completed or deleted and was last processed longer ago than the number of days specified in the retention setting. The documents last processed time is determined from TotalAgility.doc_dim.dt_last_proc_datetime.
How many documents are deleted per PurgeDocumentData iteration?
The number of documents deleted per iteration use the environment variable "PurgeFieldDataBatchSize", default 10,000.
Are there any retention related considerations while troubleshooting an issue with reporting or Insight/KAFTA?
See
Preserve data while troubleshooting a KTA Reporting processing issue

answer:
The following steps show how the Federated Security login can be bypassed when access to KTA outside of FS is required. This might be necessary, for instance if the admin login cannot be authenticated using FS or when a license server is changed and the database is still pointing to the old license server. This information is also found in the KTA Administrators Guide.
Log on to TotalAgility in Recovery Mode
To log on to TotalAgility in recovery mode, you should have full control or read write permissions. You can log on to TotalAgility in recovery mode by using the recovery mode session ID. To obtain or update the recovery mode session ID:
1. Run the
Configuration Utility*
from your installed location.
2. On the App settings tab, locate the RecoveryModeSessionId key. By default, the key has a session ID that can be used to logon. You can change the session ID as required.
3. The
Recovery Mode Session ID**
is also stored in the Web.config file from the following setting:
<add key= "RecoveryModeSessionId" value= <recovery mode session ID>/>
4. Use the following URL:
http://<server name>/TotalAgility/Designer/#/logon/recovery
to log on.
5. Enter the Recovery mode session ID and click Validate. The Recovery mode page appears.
6. Enter the Username and Password.
7. Click Login. The TotalAgility Designer is launched in the recovery mode.
* Note:
The configuration utility is available on the installation media and must be manually copied to your Kofax TotalAgility server.
** Note:
The recovery mode session ID is unique. With each installation, the value in Web.config is updated with a new ID.
8. If it was enabled and you cannot find it in Config Utility or web.config file, you can use this query to retrieve it from the database:
select
cast(server_settings as XML).value('(/ServerSettingsServerEntity/ServerSettingsServerEntity/RecoveryModeSessionId)[1]', 'varchar(max)')
as RecoveryModeSessionId
from server_data with (nolock)
In future, before enabling FS, you should get the recovery session ID from the KTA Designer > System Settings > Logon and authentication > User sessions > Recovery mode session ID (available from version 7.11).
Level of Complexity
Easy

answer:
When performing OCR with Tungsten Clarity (Google) or Azure (Microsoft), the existing page image is converted to PNG and then sent to the OCR provider.
Advanced troubleshooting
The converted PNG should be essentially identical to the original page image. But if advanced troubleshooting is needed, and there is reason to suspect a problem with the PNG sent to these services, then the intermediate PNG can be saved.
Add a folder path to an environment variable with the appropriate name:
Clarity: ClarityDumpVisionImageFilePath
Azure: AzureAIDumpImageFilePath
Then OCR requests will save the PNG to that folder. The same name is always used, overwriting if it already exists:
Clarity always saves as 
Azure always saves as 
So, for example, if both environment variables were set to C:\Temp\ and then both OCR engines were tested, and would be created.
This should only be used for troubleshooting in a test environment because this adds performance overhead.
question: What image is sent when using Clarity or Azure OCR?

answer:
Windows Authentication is
not
supported for logging into the TotalAgility OPMT Designer or Workspace, so r
unning the AD sync process in an OPMT environment is not supported either. As stated in the
Documentation
, the Integration Server does not support the Active Directory Synchronization process. From the perspective of running this on a tenant server, it would not be possible, as tenants do not have access to the hosts AD server.
Instead, the resources are presented with a manual logon form that requires their username and password.
However, users can configure Federated Security in TotalAgility OPMT. More information on Federated Security, including how the resources are created, is available here:
Federated security
That means in OPMT / TotalAgility Cloud, users can use the AD Sync process to create users. But to log on, they cannot use AD SSO. If they use Federated Security to log on, they can use the AD Sync-generated users when they match users against their email address.
This option might be preferable if they have groups configured in AD that they want to use instead of setting up custom claim rules.
Please note that t
his is not a common approach, normally custom claim rules should be used.

answer:
If data was successfully deleted, why are SQL Server data files taking the same amount of space on disk?
SQL Server does not shrink database files automatically when you've freed up space within them, because the logical assumption is that if you've used that space once, you'll use it again. Autogrow [increasing the size of the files on disk] can be an expensive event and unnecessary if you've only freed up space temporarily -
sql server - Disk space full but logical space available in database - Database Administrators Stack Exchange
If the files on disk stay the same size, how can I see how much space is actually used by data?
Microsoft discusses various methods here:
Display data & log space info for a database - SQL Server | Microsoft Learn
Additionally, this query shows the information in a clear way:
https://dba.stackexchange.com/a/343329/29254
Why do DBAs generally not recommend shrinking database files?
As mentioned above, the assumption is that the space will be needed again, and autogrow (increasing the size of the files on disk as needed) is a performance cost. If autogrow is disabled, then running out of space will cause fatal errors and possibly data loss. Shrinking database files also increases index fragmentation, which also hurts performance.
What if I want to shrink database files anyway?
Follow Microsofts guidance in either of the following articles:
Shrink a Database - SQL Server | Microsoft Learn
Shrink a File - SQL Server | Microsoft Learn
Note
This article summarizes SQL Server concepts, which are better explained in full by Microsoft, and best understood and acted upon by an experienced DBA. These concepts are not specific to TotalAgility and are presented here simply because Tungsten support is frequently asked about them.
Additionally, this article is about the situation where data is genuinely successfully deleted. To troubleshoot whether TotalAgility retention policies are working, open a support case with the Retention queries from either
Tech Support Queries Utility
or
Tech Support Queries Form.

Succinct instructions to follow when beginning troubleshooting:
TotalAgility Reporting Troubleshooting Guide
Kofax Analytics for TotalAgility (KAFTA) issues:
KAFTA does not show new data
Issues resolved in KAFTA
Using
reporting data:
Resources for Writing Custom Analytic/Reporting Queries
This rest of this article provides in-depth information about how TotalAgility reporting processing works and information that may help when troubleshooting.
Basic Flow of Reporting Data in KTA
Writing raw reporting data to the reporting staging database
All components in KTA that process activities will write raw reporting data messages into the wsa_messages table in the reporting staging database. For an on-premise installation this would default to TotalAgility_Reporting_Staging.dbo.wsa_messages, while an on-premise multitenant installation would be TotalAgility_TenantName.reportingstaging
live
.wsa_messages or TotalAgility_TenantName.reportingstaging
dev
.wsa_messages depending on whether it is a live or dev environment.
Disabling collection of raw reporting is possible as of KTA 7.9. For more details:
Disabling TotalAgility Reporting
If raw unprocessed data remains in this table past a certain point, it will be deleted. For more details:
Retaining or deleting older raw unprocessed reporting data with WsaRetentionInterval
Processing reporting messages
In the System Tasks section of the KTA Designer, there is a Reporting task which has a default schedule of every minute. When the Kofax TotalAgility Reporting service (Kofax.CEBPM.Reporting.TAService.exe) takes the available reporting task, it starts an instance of the ETL process (Kofax.CEBPM.Reporting.AzureETL.exe) to run the ETL job that processes a batch of available messages out of the wsa_messages table. Within the course of the ETL job raw data is extracted from the encrypted messages out into the other tables of the staging database, and then transforms the data into the form that is copied in the main TotalAgility_Reporting database (the data warehouse).
Data available in data warehouse database until retention period ends
The data in the TotalAgility_Reporting database is then used externally, for example, by execution plans in the KAFTA project, or by custom reporting. How long the data stays in this database is determined by specific settings and circumstances detailed in the following articles.
TotalAgility 8.0 and later:
TotalAgility Reporting Data Retention
TotalAgility 7.11 and earlier:
KTA Reporting Field Retention Period
KTA Reporting Document Data Retention
Data persists in KAFTA
Under normal operation, KAFTA loads the latest data hourly, and this data will persist in the KAFTA_Data database even when it is removed from the reporting database by retention settings. However, do not ever do a Beginning of times to Current time data load or KAFTA will lose any previously accumulated data that is not still retained in the reporting database. When using those parameters, Insight will clear all data before running the execution plan.
For TAFTA 1.5:
Review this import
Article
related to manually re-running plans.
TAFTA 1.5 requires configuring Data Retention for its data management plans. Make sure to review the
Administrator's Guide
which discusses how to configure that. Enabling these plans are required for the Hourly and Minute plans now. As well, the default retention days may not be sufficient for your particular business needs and may require updating.
Troubleshooting Concepts
Avoid a gap in reporting data while troubleshooting a problem
Because the retention period for field data is short, it could be that by the time a problem is solved, some data is immediately subject to be deleted because it is older than the data retention period. This would then lead to a gap in reporting data for that period of time that was deleted. To avoid this, take preparations before resolving the problem:
Preserve data while troubleshooting a TotalAgility Reporting processing issue
Troubleshooting Queries
A helpful way to collect a lot of information at once is to get the results of the Reporting queries using either of the following. Note that reporting logs will still also be needed for most problems.
Tech Support Queries Utility
Tech Support Queries Form
Backlog in wsa_messages
A notable type of problem is one where the reporting service is not able to successfully process raw reporting data out of the wsa_messages table, thus either no data, or no data past a certain date, will be available in the main reporting database or in KAFTA. If there are a large number of messages and the number continues to rise, then there are likely errors being written to the logs. To check for and quantify this problem, see:
Backlog of data in the TotalAgility Reporting Staging database
.
For a general picture of how much data is in what state, it can be useful to see a table size report:
Provide a Table Size Report
In situations where a large backlog has accumulated, it might be more desirable to reset the staging database that contains raw unprocessed reporting data. This would mean that old data would be lost, but it would allow new data to start processing immediately. Or it might be desirable to recreate the main reporting database if wanting to get rid of all existing processed reporting data.
Reset Reporting Staging Database
Recreate Reporting Database
Reporting logs
To increase the logging level and collect the logs, see:
Increase Reporting Logging Level
For more in-depth details:
Reporting Logging Details
Processing rate
When investigating certain issues, it can be helpful to quantify the amount and date range of data that was processed in a specific time. To do this, see:
TotalAgility Reporting Service Processing Rate
.
Specific Types of Problems
Documents marked completed
Although document data is always sent to reporting, KAFTA only uses data from documents that have been marked completed. There are
several ways
to mark a document as completed.
Timeouts
One type of problem that can prevent the reporting service from processing is if timeouts occur in certain database operations. Many such problems can be addressed by increasing the timeout. For more details see:
Increasing Timeouts used by the KTA Reporting Service DatabaseCmdTimeoutSec
KTA 7.10+:
The transaction associated with the current connection has completed but has not been disposed. The transaction must be disposed before the connection can be used to execute SQL statements.
Data different than expected
When no errors are occurring, but data in the reporting database seems different than is expected, it is best to try isolate a specific controlled action that produces different data than is expected.
Examples where there is nuance to understanding what data to expect:
TotalAgility Reporting field data: How extraction and user changes are tracked
KAFTA Extraction Statistics Report and Fields or Table Cells Not Processed Through Extraction
Others
Kofax.CEBPM.Reporting.AzureETL.exe process is consuming a lot of memory

Reporting issues resolved in KTA 7.9 Fix Packs
Reporting issues resolved in KTA 7.10 Fix Packs
Reporting issues resolved in KTA 7.11 Fix Packs

Reporting issues resolved in TotalAgility 8.1 Fix Packs

When a job suspends with this message, there should be a separate error in the Application event logs with the real error. Unfortunately, job notes will only contain the generic message. Because cloud customers cannot see logs, they cannot distinguish between different issues of this kind. Below are some of the currently known real errors and respective solutions.
Internally, cloud logs need to be searched with the following search string, and then analyze the inner exceptions:
message:"ErrorWhileImageProcessing" NOT message:"TargetInvocationException"
#1 Image source does not support multipage images
If a Gen AI activity has the Source setting set to Image, it supports only a single image (GIF/JPEG/PNG). If a multipage TIFF or a PDF is provided, then the generic error message is thrown. This is expected behavior: consider changing Source to Text. For more details:
Generative AI activity to support multipage image PDF/TIFF/GIF/JPEG/PNG
Generative AI activity : Error occurred while processing the document. Either the document failed to retrieved from the repository or it is not supported.
#2 An invalid request URI was provided. The request URI must either be an absolute URI or BaseAddress must be set.
Fixed in 8.1.0.11, 2025.2.2, and 2026.1 (Bug 2211474)
Occurs when a Gen AI activity tries to perform OCR with Azure OCR. Workaround is to set OmniPage as a fallback profile or make sure that a previous activity has already run OCR.
#3 An item with the same key has already been added
Error while processing generate narrative text request: Kofax.CEBPM.CPUServer.Common.Exceptions.CpuServerException: An item with the same key has already been added.
Fixed in 8.1.0.11 (Bug 2210097) and 2025.2 (Bug 2177759)
#4 Parsing/escaping errors
Fixed in 8.1.0.11(indirectly via bug 2212964) and 2025.2 (Bug 2154215)
Specific error messages can vary such as Bad JSON or After parsing a value an unexpected character was encountered
#5 Failed to generate narrative text from document - [05003] The rectangle is invalid
Fixed in 2026.1.3 (Bug 2212214) and 2026.2 (Bug 2217635)
#6 NullReferenceException
Occurs when a key value pair has a value with no word objects. Fixed in 2026.2 (Bug 2229926)
Failed to generate narrative text from document 7ba4bea8-c027-4e67-9fcf-b3f0004be9a9: System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
at Kofax.CEBPM.DataLayer.Extensions.KvpExtensions.ToJson(XElement element, List`1& words)
at Kofax.CEBPM.DataLayer.Extensions.KvpExtensions.PairToJson(XElement element, List`1& words)
at Kofax.CEBPM.DataLayer.Extensions.KvpExtensions.GetKeyValuePairsJsonFromPredictionsXml(IDocument document)
at Kofax.CEBPM.CPUServer.Core.ResourceServer.ExecutionModel.ExecutionDocument.NarratorDocumentExecution.Execute(IServiceProcess serviceProcess)
#7 Activity time out due to noisy image
Fix 2026.2.1 and 2026.3 (Bug 2234572)
This fix improves line detection speed and reduces unwanted vertical lines in areas like photos and graphics, thus making it less likely a timeout will occur on noisy images.
Failed to generate narrative text from document a25b4f3b-5e0c-4d8e-a400-b405009c868f: Kofax.CEBPM.CPUServer.Common.Exceptions.CpuServerException: Activity timed out.
at Kofax.CEBPM.CPUServer.Core.ResourceServer.ProcessPools.ServiceProcess`1.RunTaskExecution[T](Func`1 serviceCall)
at Kofax.CEBPM.CPUServer.Core.ResourceServer.ExecutionModel.ExecutionDocument.NarratorDocumentExecution.Execute(IServiceProcess serviceProcess)
The activity timeout itself means that the MaxSyncProcessingDurationInSeconds timeout was breached before the narrative text could be generated and could potentially still happen in other unexpected situations.
#8 SetAzureAiContext binding error
This error was observed but resolved on it's own before further information could be gathered:
The message with Action 'TransformationDesignService/ITransformationDesignService/SetAzureAiContext' cannot be processed at the receiver, due to a ContractFilter mismatch at the EndpointDispatcher. This may be because of either a contract mismatch (mismatched Actions between sender and receiver) or a binding/security mismatch between the sender and the receiver. Check that sender and receiver have the same contract and the same binding (including security requirements, e.g. Message, Transport, None).
The generic message itself
Additionally, a bug has been submitted regarding the message itself, as it would avoid confusion about which issue occurred if the real error messages are displayed:
Bug 2220891:Generic message hides real errors from Gen AI activity: Error occurred while processing the document. Either the document failed to retrieved from the repository or it is not supported..

answer:
When something is added to a normal package, all associated items are added. By contrast, a patch package includes only manually added items, with no associated items included.
When to avoid patch packages
To best understand when a patch package might be used, its important to first understand where it cannot or should not be used.
New Environments:
It cant be used to move a solution to a new environment (where it does not already exist). In most cases, missing associated items will prevent the package from importing at all. In some cases, the package may import but then fail to open either in the designer or runtime.
Technical Support:
For the same reason above, patch packages cannot be used to provide items to technical support. Even after previously providing a normal package, if another is needed, its best to continue to use normal packages, especially because cases may be transferred to other members of technical support or need to be tested in new environments. Providing patch packages in support cases will cause delays.
Smaller Packages:
Although patch packages will inherently be smaller than a full package, there are other options that dont have the same limitations and pitfalls.
Exclude training data:
The primary driver of large packages is training sets, and these can be excluded to avoid the extra size:
Exclude training sets from TotalAgility Package
Default package type:
Even when prompting updates between environments that already have the same solution, using patch packages allows the possibility of mismatched artifacts. The default choice should be a normal package. Only use patch packages when there is a clear need, and the person making the choice has an in-depth understanding of the solution.
When to us patch packages
A developer would use a patch package when they have a full understanding of their solution and a dev environment with items ready to be promoted, but with associated items that are not ready to be promoted.
Example: Imagine that the name of an existing menu item in a navigation needs to be changed and this change needs to be promoted from dev into higher environments. If you add a navigation to a normal package, it will add all of the forms linked by the navigation as associated items. If these forms have changes that are not yet ready to be promoted out of dev, then a patch package could be used to add only the navigation and avoid including the associated forms.
However, the developer making this choice must have an in-depth understanding of the solution. For example, if the navigation had also been updated to link to a new form that is still in development, the patch package avoids promoting the form, but the navigation will still have that broken link when prompted.
Using a normal package like a patch package
A normal package already contains associated items, but you have the ability to choose not to import them, effectively using it like a patch package.
See the Exclude associations section of
Import a package
Determine package type from existing zip file
If you already have a package file and are unsure of the type, open the zip file and open the PackageInfo.xml file within.
Under SpaPackageImportExportData > PackageSummary> SpaPackageType, the value will be Default for a normal package, or PatchPackage for a patch package.
History
Patch package functionality was added in TotalAgility 8.0.
In earlier versions, there was not a need for patch packages, because it was possible to create normal packages without associations, either intentionally or accidentally.
Those changes to normal packages are described here:
How "Include Items Associated with Processes" works when creating a package
Related:
Package creation performance in 7.11

answer:
Option 1 - Kofax VRS Elite Twain-On-Top
Tungsten VRS Elite Twain-on-Top (ToT) should be used as the scan source rather than direct TWAIN from the Scan Create New Job (SCNJ) form. The highest performance can only be achieved with VRS Elite,
especially
if image enhancement is needed at scan time.
Option 2 - Scan Agent Service
If the network bandwidth between the scan client and the TotalAgility server is low, the Scan Agent Service (SAS) should be leveraged. This is a separate component that can be installed on the scan client. It uploads the images to TotalAgility asynchronously, allowing a scan user to proceed with scanning the next batch.
Option 3 - Chunked Uploads
When scanning in color, images will naturally be larger. This can add overhead when uploading the images to TotalAgility. The settings below can help reduce this overhead.
Maximum binary chunk size
Determines the maximum size of an image before it is broken into chunks.
Maximum number of upload requests
Determines the number of chunks that should be concurrently uploaded to the KTA server
For example, the chunk size can be set to 1 MB (1024 KB), and upload requests can be set to 10. A 20 MB image will be split into 20 chunks of 1 MB each. A maximum of 10 chunks will be uploaded to TotalAgility concurrently at a given time. Subsequently, the chunks will be merged on the server once all have been uploaded.
Option 4 - Linked Servers
Linked servers can be useful when scanning from one location to another. For example, when the scan clients are in Paris but the TotalAgility server is in New York. A TotalAgility server would be installed on the Paris site, which scans clients connecting to. The TotalAgility server hosted in Paris would send the documents to the TotalAgility server hosted in New York using the Transfer activity.

answer:
The TotalAgility SDK is the same set of API functions that can be called via JSON or .NET. When using the API via .NET, IntelliSense and compiler errors make it easy to provide structurally correct parameters. However, manually crafting a JSON request/payload is tedious and can easily lead to mistakes.
As of TotalAgility 8.0, the designer includes tools to help with
JSON payload generation
. In addition to helpers within a process (
Generate the JSON payload
), a general-purpose tool to generate request payloads for any API function (
Generate the TotalAgility SDK JSON payload
). Click TotalAgility SDK JSON payload under Integration to access.
JSON SDK Helper
is a separate utility with similar core functionality. It includes a few extra conveniences, but is mostly useful in versions older than 8.0, which do not have this functionality built in.
Note that the TotalAgility SDK is different from the TotalAgility REST API which is a separate streamlined set of functions introduced in 8.0 (
RESTful service
). A local
Swagger
interface is provided to document and test the REST API. Click TotalAgility REST API under Integration to access.
question: JSON payload generation

answer:
TotalAgility 8.0 and higher includes built-in tools to help with
JSON payload generation
. The utility below includes a few extra conveniences such as prepopulating the system session ID and allowing in-place testing, but is mostly useful in versions older than 8.0, which do not have this functionality built in.
KTA Package:
JsonSdkHelper.zip
The included form allows selecting any TA API function to provide a correctly formatted JSON request. Edit as needed and test calling the function.
This is intended to programmatically create
structurally correct
JSON requests for API functions to avoid structure and spelling mistakes inherent in the tedious process of hand-crafting JSON by looking at documentation.
This does not necessarily mean these auto-generated requests are valid for the actual API as-is. Refer to API documentation for the specific function for actual valid parameter combinations and values.
Usage
Import the attached package (check the Overwrite option in case of a previous version)
In the workspace, open JsonSdk.form
Select SDK Service and function to generate JSON
Edit request JSON as needed and click to call client side through JavaScript, or server side through .NET HttpClient.
Simple Example
For SDK Service, select TotalAgility.Sdk.UserService
For Function, select
GetLoggedOnUsers2
This function is ready to use as-is because it only takes a session ID parameter, which is generated pre-populated
Click either of the call buttons to see the API response
Elaborate Example
For SDK Service, select TotalAgility.Sdk.JobService
For Function, select
GetJobs4
This function takes an elaborate filter object that can have several types of nested objects, so the generated request is quite long.
Calling as-is results in an error, and you would need to use the documentation to determine what parameters are required and what values to those parameters are valid and logical.
Despite the large number of possible parameters on the filter object, a valid request can be made by replacing the whole filter object with just the following:
"jobFilter": {
 "MaxNumberToRetrieve": 1
}
Technical Details
Because the function lists are programmatically generated, they include all functions even if undocumented. However these should not be used because only documented functions are supported.
The auto generated JSON request has parameters set to default values for the data type, with the following additions:
 Collection types have one item of that type added
 DateTime is set to the current moment
 sessionId parameters are automatically populated with a session ID

answer:
TotalAgility 7.11 and earlier:
Generally, no, see
TotalAgility 7.11 and earlier: Running Multiple TotalAgility Reporting Services
TotalAgility 8.0 and later:
Yes: no special configuration is required, and this article just provides additional technical details.
With the SQL Server Application Lock (AppLock) mechanism, multiple TotalAgility Reporting Services can now run safely. The AppLock provides database-level protection against concurrent processing conflicts, eliminating the risk of deadlocking and data corruption.
Architecture
Reporting data in a TotalAgility system or tenant cannot be processed by multiple reporting services at once. Because of this, adding more reporting services does not scale the processing ability, and the only potential benefit is redundancy. In a multi-tenant system, multiple services can allow processing of reporting data for more tenants at once. The number of ETL processes per service is controlled by the
ETLPoolCapacity
config file setting, which defaults to 3.
The SQL Server Application Lock (AppLock) mechanism now provides protection against concurrent processing conflicts. When a reporting service attempts to start ETL processing, it acquires an exclusive lock on a resource named
TenantCode + EnvironmentName
. If another service attempts to process the same tenant simultaneously, the lock acquisition fails after a 5-second timeout, and the ETL process exits gracefully without causing database deadlocking or data corruption.
With AppLock protection, running multiple reporting services is now safe and offers:
Redundancy
- Multiple services can pick up tasks if one fails
Horizontal Scaling (multi-tenant)
- Different tenants can be processed in parallel across services
Safe Failover
- AppLock prevents data corruption during failover scenarios
No Deadlocking
- Database-level lock prevents concurrent access to the same tenant data
Each tenant has its own independent lock, enabling true parallel processing across tenants while still protecting individual tenants from concurrent access.
Reporting System Task
When the reporting system task is available (standalone system or individual tenant), a reporting service will take the task and start the
Kofax.CEBPM.Reporting.AzureETL.exe
process to work through one iteration of reporting data processing, then the process will close, and the task will be marked completed. The task will be available again on the reporting system task interval, which is usually every minute.
As long as the reporting task is taken by a reporting service, no other reporting services will try to process data for that system/tenant. It stays taken until the iteration of data is processed, or until it hits the task timeout, which defaults to 30 minutes.
Reporting data processing is performance-intensive and can take a long time; as a result, there can be situations where it can exceed the default task timeout. When the task times out, it is reset and becomes available for any reporting service to pick up. The reporting service that is still processing will still hold the AppLock, so if another reporting service takes the task, it won't be able to take the AppLock and fails gracefully.
Application Lock Protection
How AppLock Works
When an ETL process starts, it acquires a SQL Server application lock:
Lock Resource = TenantCode + EnvironmentName
Lock Mode = Exclusive
Lock Timeout = 5 seconds
Lock Behavior:
Scenario
Behavior
First ETL starts
Acquires lock successfully
Second ETL for same tenant
Waits 5 seconds, then fails gracefully
ETL for different tenant
Acquires separate lock, runs in parallel
ETL completes
Lock released automatically
Warning but no problems: Single reporting service
After a reporting task has timed out and reset, then it is available to be picked up again. If the same service picks up the task, it knows that it is already working on it and logs the following warning in the WorkerRole.log file.
Received task for tenant Non-multitenant environment which is currently being processing. Completing.
With AppLock protection, this warning is now harmless even with multiple services.
If a different reporting service picks up the timed-out task, the AppLock mechanism will prevent it from processing the same data concurrently. The second service will wait 5 seconds for the lock, fail to acquire it, and exit gracefully with a warning log entry.
Safe Concurrent Processing: Multiple Reporting Services with AppLock
The AppLock mechanism eliminates the severe problems that previously occurred when multiple reporting services attempted to process data at the same time.
Previously (without AppLock):
Multiple services could process the same data simultaneously
This caused catastrophic blocking/deadlocking in the database
Risk of duplicate or corrupt reporting data
Now (with AppLock):
Only one service can hold the lock per tenant at a time
Second service waits 5 seconds, then exits gracefully
No database deadlocking occurs
No data corruption risk
Lock Failure Handling
When AppLock cannot be acquired, the ETL process logs a warning and exits:
Application lock hasn't acquired. Return code:-1, stop execution
Unable get application lock to start ETL execution
This is
expected behavior
and does not indicate a problem. It simply means another ETL process is already handling that tenant's data.

question: TotalAgility 7.11 and earlier: Only Run One Reporting Service Per Installation

answer:
TotalAgility 8.0 and later:
There is no issue with running multiple Reporting services. For details, see:
Running Multiple TotalAgility Reporting Services
TotalAgility 7.11 and earlier:
the guidance in this article applies. To avoid severe problems, the general advice for most systems is to
Only Run One TotalAgility Reporting Service Per Installation
.
To understand why, or when installing an environment that will attempt to run multiple reporting servers, several concepts must be understood and settings must be changed.
Architecture
Reporting data in a KTA system is not able to be processed by multiple reporting services at once. Because of this, adding more reporting services does not scale the processing ability and the only potential benefit is redundancy. But there are pitfalls that must be understood and handled by settings changes.
Multitenant Architecture
Reporting data in an individual tenant is not able to be processed by multiple reporting services at once. But multiple services can allow processing reporting data for more tenants at once. The number of ETL processes per service is controlled by the ETLPoolCapacity config file setting, which defaults to 3.
Running multiple reporting services in a multitenant system offers redundancy and horizontal scaling. But there are pitfalls that must be understood and handled by settings changes.
Controlling processing via the system task
When the reporting system task is available (standalone system or individual tenant), a reporting service will take the task and start the Kofax.CEBPM.Reporting.AzureETL.exe process to work through one iteration of reporting data processing, then the process will close, and the task will be marked completed. The task will be available again on the reporting system task interval which is usually every minute.
As long as the reporting task is taken by a reporting service, no other reporting services will try to process data for that system/tenant. It stays taken as long as it takes to process that iteration of data, or until it hits the task timeout. In fresh installs of 7.11 and higher, the default reporting task timeout is 30 minutes. In earlier versions or upgrades from earlier versions, it was 5 minutes.
Reporting data processing is performance intensive and can take a long time, and as such it is not uncommon for it to take longer than the default task timeout. When the task times out, it gets reset and is available for any reporting service to pick up, the reporting service is already processing data and continues processing.
Warning, but no problems: Single reporting service
After a reporting task has timed out and reset, then it is available to be picked up again. If the same service picks up the task, it knows that it is already working on it and logs the below warning in the WorkerRole.log file. In a system where there is only one reporting service, this might happen multiple times, and ultimately it does not cause any problems. However, in a system with multiple services, seeing this message means it is very likely that you are also already experiencing the problems in the next section.
Received task for tenant Non-multitenant environment which is currently being processing. Completing.
Severe Problems: Multiple reporting services are attempting to process data at the same time
If a different reporting service picks up the timed-out/reset task, this different reporting service does not have any mechanism to know that the first service is still processing. At this point multiple services actually try to process the same data at the same time, which can cause catastrophic blocking/deadlocking in the database. It also opens the possibility of causing duplicate or corrupt reporting data.
Implications
When running multiple reporting services, the only way to prevent this situation is to increase the reporting system task timeout to a value higher than it would ever take to process one iteration of reporting data processing. Since it is not possible to predict exactly how long each iteration might take, the value should just be set significantly higher.
The downside of a longer task timeout: If a reporting service ran into a problem while processing, then it would take until the end of the task timeout before processing would be attempted again.
In versions earlier than 7.10.0.5, some circumstances could lead to one iteration taking substantially longer, thus in these versions we would strongly recommend not using multiple reporting services.
Changing the Reporting system task timeout
If you wish to run multiple reporting services without the pitfalls described above, you must increase the task timeout:
Open KTA Designer > System > System Settings > System tasks > Reporting
Change the task timeout interval to a high number like 12 hours.
In a multitenant system, this is set per tenant.

Question / Problem:
How to reopen a closed Support Case?
Answer / Solution:
Support Cases are closed sometimes automatically by the Support Portal Software. This happens if, after a certain period, no feedback is received from our customer. Closed Cases can be reopened 14 days after that they have been closed. If the Case is older then 14 days, you will have to open a new Case. You can refer to the old Case by the old Case Number.

Increase TotalAgility Reporting timeout - DatabaseCmdTimeoutSec
Increase TotalAgility Reporting bulk copy timeout

Preserve data while troubleshooting a TotalAgility Reporting processing issue

answer:
Document images that are very large can end up causing many different issues: memory errors, long processing times, display issues.
How does this happen?
Aside from special situations like scanning legitimately large architectural drawings, this often happens with document images that are
unintentionally
very large. A common scenario that causes this is when document images originate from a mobile device rather than a scanner. The physical paper shown in the image might be a standard letter size, but unlike a scanner, the mobile device does not know how big the object is and cannot put an accurate DPI value in the image file. Because of the incorrect DPI headers, the file has a much larger print size than the paper it is meant to represent. Incorrect DPI headers can then cause file sizes to get inflated further when converting between formats or using image processing.
It is also important to understand that this kind of problem can cause an image to be inflated to a size that the Transformation components cannot support, which can result in errors. For details on limits, see
Image files
.
How to handle images like this:
Use a Document Conversion activity with a profile that uses the Scale To setting. As an example, this setting can be set to Letter (8.5" x 11"). After processing with this profile, the document will have the correct print size and DPI headers and thus avoid a large number of possible problems.

The TotalAgility API can be accessed via WCF rather than by referencing TotalAgility.Sdk.dll. Unlike using TotalAgility.Sdk.dll, using the API via WCF works in sandboxed multi-tenant environments.
WCF clients are the classes automatically generated by Visual Studio or Svcutil.exe. They also might be referred to as Service References, Connected Services, WCF proxies, or WCF Web Services. This article assumes youve already generated WCF clients for the TotalAgility services you want to use. For more info about WCF clients, and generating them in Visual Studio, see:
Add, update, or remove WCF data service reference - Visual Studio (Windows) | Microsoft Learn
WCF Client Overview - WCF | Microsoft Learn
WCF clients need to be initialized with the url of the service and the correct bindings that match the server. Usually, WFC bindings and endpoints are defined in config files. However, custom DLLs developed for use in TotalAgility must be self-contained: they dont have their own config files and cannot modify the TotalAgility product config files. Thus, bindings and endpoints need to be initialized programmatically. Below is a sample helper method that returns WCF client classes with the correct bindings.
Other than TA-specific service names, this is mostly general WCF development knowledge, and it is the developer's responsibility to understand any code they use and to determine whether it meets their needs. Using this sample code is not a replacement for understanding WCF.
Example usage:
As can be seen in the function, the SDK URL (like
https://localhost/TotalAgility/Services/SDK/
) does need to be provided. This should not be hardcoded as that would prevent testing the code in other environments. Thus, it makes sense that it should be an input to your functions, likely stored in a global or system variable.

answer:
Event Logs
With either situation, more information should be logged in the event logs, or make the web.config change below to display errors directly in the browser.
Display errors in the browser
Default settings show the generic ErrorPage out of an abundance of caution because error messages can reveal information about the system. However when troubleshooting or in dev systems, it can be helpful to allow the real errors to be displayed directly in the browser.
Open C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\Agility.Server.Web\web.config
Find the following line:
<customErrors defaultRedirect="ErrorPage.aspx?error=1" mode="On" />
Change On to Off
Now the real error is displayed
Common causes
Although you would need to see the real underlying error to troubleshoot, these are some common causes:
Web.config CoreServicesLocation is incorrect or not accessible for network reasons
This applies to Integration Servers connecting to Cloud/OPMT systems
Also applies to Web servers connecting to App servers
Web.config connection strings are incorrect or inaccessible

answer:
To reset all processed reporting data, the TotalAgility_Reporting database can be recreated. To instead reset a backlog of unprocessed reporting data, see
Reset Reporting Staging Database
To avoid needing to update database connection strings across the whole installation, it is ideal to create the new database with the same name as the original. One way to accomplish this is to drop or renaming the original database before creating the new one. Another way is to initially create the new database with a different name, then change the names of both when ready to switch.
Preparation: TotalAgility_Reporting Database
The TotalAgility Reporting Service writes to the TotalAgility_Reporting database, and when using Insight/KAFTA, the Insight services read from it. These all need to be stopped until the new database is available.
Creating and Initializing TotalAgility_Reporting Tables
After a DBA creates a new database, the tables can be created and initialized by running the scripts that are included in the KTA install source.
{Install Source}\TotalAgility\TotalAgilityInstall\DatabaseScripts\SQL Server\Reporting\Kofax.Reporting.Analytics.SQL_TablesCreate.sql
{Install Source}\TotalAgility\TotalAgilityInstall\DatabaseScripts\SQL Server\Reporting\Kofax Kofax.Reporting.Analytics.SQL_Initialize.sql
Version Specific Steps
If the KTA installation is on a Service Pack/Fix Pack that has made changes to these databases, then those changes need to be applied as well. In other words, run any scripts included with the fix pack install files from base version to fix pack version.
KTA 7.10
If your major version is 7.10 and you are in fix pack 5 or higher, then run this:
{Fix Pack Install Source}\TotalAgilityInstall\DatabaseScripts\SQL Server\Upgrade Database Scripts\7.10.0.0 To 7.10.0.5 Conversion Scripts\Kofax.Reporting.Analytics.SQL_Convert.sql
Additionally if you are in fix pack 8 or higher, then run this:
{Fix Pack Install Source}\TotalAgilityInstall\DatabaseScripts\SQL Server\Upgrade Database Scripts\7.10.0.5 To 7.10.0.7 Conversion Scripts\Kofax.Reporting.Analytics.SQL_Convert.sql
KTA 7.5
If your major version is 7.5 and you are in fix pack 5 or higher, then run this:
{Fix Pack Install Source}\TotalAgilityInstall\DatabaseScripts\SQL Server\Upgrade Database Scripts\7.5.0.0 To 7.5.0.5 Conversion Scripts\Kofax.Reporting.Analytics.SQL_Convert.sql
KTA 7.3
If your major version is 7.3 and you are in fix pack 2 or higher (any service pack is higher than this), then run this:
{Fix Pack Install Source}\\TotalAgilityInstall\DatabaseScripts\SQL Server\Upgrade Database Scripts\ 7.3.0.2 To 7.3.1.0 Conversion Scripts\Kofax.Reporting.Analytics.SQL_Convert.sql
7.10.0.5 and earlier: Before Restarting Services
Often if this database is being recreated, then so is the staging database (see
Reset Reporting Staging Database
). However if you are recreating only the main Reporting database, and keeping a staging database that has unprocessed reporting data, then run the following update on the TotalAgility_Reporting_Staging database,
after
the Reporting database has been recreated, but
before
the reporting service is started. This allows the data remaining in the staging database to properly process into the newly created main database.
UPDATE kc_batch_action SET etl_job_key=-1
UPDATE batch_edit SET etl_job_key=-1
Level of Complexity
Moderate

Let's say you're designing a capture project in TD on an OP environment. Your Databases/Dictionaries are in the below locations:
C:\Databases
C:\Dictionaries
Whenever the Databases or Dictionaries are added to the capture project, they are added to the project's cached folder structure. This is the location the project is downloaded to when opening. When the project is released, the project will be uploaded to the KTA DB along with the custom Databases and Dictionaries. Therefore, when the project is added to a package the Databases and Dictionaries are included.
As a result, these will be included when the package is imported into their SaaS environment.
What I have said above works well given the Databases or Dictionaries are static. However, if the files will be changed dynamically then obviously we will want KTA to pick up these changes. We do this by using the "Automatic update from import file" option. Since we cannot use a file location, you would need to use a Web location so the updated Database/Dictionary can be accessed in Azure.
The "Automatic update from import file" setting is available when adding a Dictionary or Database to the capture project. See screenshots below. When using a web location a URL is used instead of a windows file path.
Additional details can be found in the KTA documentation
https://docshield.tungstenautomation.com/KTA/en_US/8.1.0-rmx0b1ux3q/help/TransformationDesigner/ProjectBuilder/100_UserInterface/ProjectSettings/r_WebResourceWindow.html

In general, it is not supported to query the TotalAgility product databases, as explained in the topic
Querying the TotalAgility Databases
. However, there is a subset of tables and columns that are documented and supported specifically for use in the context of analytics. The following product documentation links are version-specific, but documents for any other version can be found at the
full documentation list
.
TotalAgility database
Tables for Analytics
 main TotalAgility database - describes the Kofax TotalAgility tables and fields used by Kofax Analytics for TotalAgility. As the document shows in more detail, the following are the only tables that are supported.
CATEGORY
AW_RESOURCE
AW_LOGGEDON_USER
AUDIT_LOG
BUSINESS_PROCESS
LIVE_ACTIVITY
LIVE_ACTIVITY_RESOURCE
JOB
JOB_HISTORY
JOB_MILESTONES
JOB_STATE_HISTORY
FINISHED_JOB
FINISHED_JOB_HISTORY
FINISHED_JOB_MILESTONES
FINISHED_JOB_STATE_HISTORY
FINISHED_JOB_META_DATA
WORK_QUEUE_DEFINITION
WORK_QUEUE_DEFINITION_FIELD
LIVE_WORK_QUEUE_DEFINITION
TotalAgility_Reporting database
Reporting Tables
 TotalAgility_Reporting database - describes the Dimension and Fact tables of the Kofax Reporting data warehouse.
Reporting Views
 TotalAgility_Reporting database - describes the sample views provided with Kofax Reporting
Kofax Analytics for TotalAgility (KAFTA) database
If using Kofax Analytics for TotalAgility (KAFTA), there is a chapter of the features guide that gives documentation for record definitions. These can either be used as base records in Insight to use when building new records, or the underlying tables can be queried in the KAFTA_Data database.
Kofax Analytics for TotalAgility Product Features Guide
 Chapter 4 Record Definitions
Undocumented tables or columns
These documents serve as the data dictionaries for the tables and columns that are supported for queries. If a table or column is not included in these documents, that means it is not supported for use.
Performance
Be aware that it is up to the author of on any custom queries to assess their performance impact. This is especially important with queri,es the TotalAgility tables used for live jobs and activities.
Querying data not normally included in the reporting database
The TotalAgility_Reporting database is document-centric and therefore does not include data such as job variables or folder fields. In some cases it may be desirable to use this type of information in queries.
One approach to making this data available would be to write data out to a custom database during the course of a job, and then query the custom database. Another approach would be to modify the process to add the desired data into a document field, which causes it to end up in the document field data in the reporting database.
Assistance in writing queries
Technical Support can assist with specific questions about the documented tables and columns, but not with writing or reviewing custom queries.
If you need a query to accomplish a specific purpose and not able to write it yourself, we suggest engaging with
Tungsten Professional Services
.
Level of Complexity
High

It is
not
supported to import a KTA package that was created in a higher version of the product to an environment with a lower version installed due to compatibility issues that would occur between the environments and the associated databases.
A package created in a lower version, though, can be imported into an environment with the same or higher installed version. The imported items will be converted to the higher version when imported.

answer:
Upgrade Schedule
How often is the TotalAgility Cloud upgraded?
The following
KB
article documents the current policy for upgrading the TA Cloud environment.
It is not mandatory that with every TotalAgility patch release, a new version of integration server or transformation designer should be released. However, if a patch includes an upgraded version of a component used by either the Integration Server or Transformation Designer, this will require the on-premise version of the component to also be upgraded. Therefore it will be announced on the Cloud Status page when a scheduled fix pack will require an upgrade of the Integration Server or Transformation Designer.
How do you subscribe to updates?
All notifications of upcoming scheduled upgrades are posted to the Cloud Status Page
https://status.tungstencloud.com/.
To receive automatic update notifications using the "Subscribe to Updates" button at the top of this page.
It will also state if an upgrade will require the OnPremise Integration Server and/or Transformation Designer to be upgraded.
OnPremise Components
Why does a Cloud customer need to install an Integration Server or Transformation Designer on a local server?
Integration Server
The TotalAgility Integration Server enables customers to utilize on-premise LOB applications within TotalAgility Azure. You can configure various LOB Connectors within the TotalAgility Designer, similar to TotalAgility On-Premise, and set the new nodes to execute either on the Azure tenant or the Integration Server.
Transformation Designer
This is the main design application for configuration of classification and data extraction. It is also used for benchmarking and testing.
TD is also used to create ML models. No data is stored locally indefinitely other than the users test documents that they manage locally. Training samples and all other configuration settings are stored in the TotalAgility databases.
TD works with a large number of files and document images therefore running this application on-premise is very efficient as it can manage large amounts of files and large fuzzy DB files, or ML models on the local file system, and only sync them back with TotalAgility when explicitly saving the project.
Why is it important that these components be upgraded at the same time?
Integration Server
As per the following
KB
article, depending on the version of the main Azure/OPMT server, the on-premise Integration Server may or may not need to be upgraded. This only applies to different TA fix pack versions,.
For each major product release, the Integration Server must be upgraded.
The Integration Server communicates with the target server via core services, therefore in previous versions of TA, if a product patch required a change to the signature of any of those services, it would also require a corresponding patch for the Integration Sever pointing to it. From TA 8.0, we now support patching the TA Azure environment without having to patch the corresponding Integration Servers.
Therefore it will always be communicated on the main Tungsten Automation Cloud Status Page if this will require an upgrade of the on-premise Integration server.
Transformation Server
In previous versions, such as v8.1. you could connect the Transformation Designer to a Transformation server of the same
major.minor
version, e.g. 8.1.x.x. Therefore, it was supported to have TD and TS on different fix pack versions, but not major versions.
In 2025.2 it was further enhanced to enable the Transformation Designer to be compatible with a higher major.minor version to allow better flexibility during upgrade schedules, when it is expected that the lower environments will be of a higher version, until all environments have been successfully upgraded.
Now in v2025.2, it is supported to configure a v2025.2 TD to point to a v2026.1 Transformation server. This allows customers to continue using their existing version of TD even when the Transformation Server has been upgraded. e.g. The DEV environment is upgraded to v2026.1, but DEV2 and PROD environments are still version v2025.2, and the TD version is v2025.2. It is supported to point your TD on v2025.2 to the DEV environment which is now v2026.1.
If TD is connected to a server with a higher major.minor version, it can only open projects that were last saved with the same or older version of TD. Projects created with the new, higher server version, cannot be opened in an older version of TD.
Currently, it is supported to connect TD to one higher major.minor version, as this functionality is only intended to be used in the short term during an upgrade phase. For example, it is supported to point a v8.1.0.5 TD to a v2025.2 Transformation Server, or a v2025.2 TD to a v2026.1 Transformation server.
During an upgrade of a tenant database in TA Azure, the tenant will be disabled, therefore TD will be unable to access the database during this scheduled downtime. Once the tenant has been upgraded, TD will inform the user when they try to access the designer, if there has been a significant change, and prompt the end user to upgrade to a compatible version of the TA server.
If using a single instance of TD to point to multiple environments, our recommendation is that the Transformation Designer equals the highest version of TotalAgility in terms of
major.minor
version. Please refer to the following
KB
article for further information.
*NOTE:
During the upgrade phase, as the lower environments will be of a higher version than Production, it is advised not to promote packages during this phase until all environments are of the same version.
Installing OnPremise Components
What are the steps to install the Integration Server?
There are two methods to install an Integration Server, either a standard install using an installation wizard or a silent install which can be used for multiple servers that use the same configuration. For further information, p
lease refer to
Chapter 2: TotalAgility Integration Sever Installation
in the
TotalAgility Integration Server Installation Guide
.
What are the steps to install the Transformation Designer?
The Transformation Designer is typically installed as part of a full Kofax TotalAgility installation. However, if you did not use the full installer or want to install Transformation Designer separately, you can use the standalone Transformation Designer installer that is available from the Kofax TotalAgility installation files. Again there are two methods available, either a standard or silent install.
Please refer to the
Chapter 4: Transformation Designer Installation
in the
TotalAgility Integration Sever Installation Guide
.
How to upgrade OnPremise Components
What are the steps to upgrade the Integration Server?
The Integration Server is upgraded in the same manner as installing the integration server; namely using either the interactive installation wizard or using a silent install. The steps to perform either a standard or silent upgrade are documented in
Chapter 14: Upgrade Process
in the
TotalAgility Integration Server Installation Guide
.
What are the steps to upgrade the Transformation Designer?
You can use the same steps as installing Transformation Designer to apply a fix pack over top of a compatible Transformation Designer installation. Please refer to the
Chapter 4: Transformation Designer Installation
in the
TotalAgility Integration Sever Installation Guide
.
Smoke Test
What is a good smoke test once the upgrade of the Transformation Designer is complete?
Firstly ensure the upgrade completes successfully without any errors.
Open the Transformation Designer to ensure it can still successfully connect to the server.
Create a new test project and ensure that it can be saved and closed without error.
What is a good smoke test once the upgrade of the Integration Server is complete?
Ensure the upgrade completes successfully without any errors.
Open the Designer and ensure it can successfully connect to the server.
Create a new test process and ensure that it can be saved and released without error.
Once the components have been upgraded successfully without error, and the application can be launched and accessed without error, we strongly recommend that a full solution test is performed. Testing helps to ensure any updates do not unexpectedly break other areas of the solution.
You can create your own test plan or create a test plan in Total Agility which allows you save test scripts against your artifacts such as processes, cases, case fragments, business rules, and custom services. You can create a test suite to execute the test plan for one or more artifacts. For further information please refer to
Test Plan
in the Kofax TotalAgility Designer Help.
Unsuccessful Upgrade
What if the upgrade was unsuccessful or there are errors reported?
Raise a new support case with Technical Support confirming the tenant environment details, and provide all relevant information relating to the error, including log files/screenshots etc.
Auto Scaling
Auto scaling works in two ways.
First is CPU and/or memory. If either of those hits 100% and remains there for more than 5 minutes, it instigates the autoscale.
Secondly there are monitors that detect if there is a backlog of jobs starting to queue, which also instigates the autoscale.
On average, it takes 15-20 minutes for Web and Core Workers to scale and 35 minutes for Transformation roles to scale.
What if you require to scale up the environment?
If a customer knows that they are adding additional volume, like a new project, they can inform Technical Support by raising a support case, and TS will inform Cloud Services who can scale up in advance. In this instance, Cloud Services will set the minimum number of roles to be higher as increased volume is expected therefore the system won't scale down as low.

answer:
Issue:
How can I resolve an issue where a job fails with the following error?
Error Description : Error. Capture variable CURRENTDOCUMENT is not initialized..
Solution:
In previous versions of KTA, the document variable used in a loop was incorrectly left initialized after the loop had terminated (with the last documentId found in that loop) - this was fixed thus the error is valid.
More specifically, this means, there was a defect identified in 7.8 (your previous version) that was addressed in 7.10, in relation to document variable property after the loop had ended.
This may have worked in v7.8, but only the last document in the folder would have been checked (this behavior was a defect).
So, you will need revisit the loop logic in the affected node to resolve this error which was identified as a defect in KTA 7.8 and was addressed in KTA 7.10.

The Transformation Designer log files are located in the user temp area; - %root%\Users\[User]\AppData\Local\Temp/. This article outlines how you can enable additional logging if you are required to troubleshoot an issue relating to Transformation Designer.
How to Enable Additional Logging?
For TA Versions Prior to 8:
Open the Kofax.ProjectBuilder.exe.config file located under:<InstallDirectory>\Kofax\TotalAgility\Transformation Designer\.
Add the following to the <system.diagnostics> section:
Restart the Transformation Designer.
Replicate the issue.
Review the TransConf_tracing_output.svclog log file.
For TA Version 8+:
In TA version 8 and above, the logging configuration has changed. Follow these updated steps:
Open the Kofax.ProjectBuilder.exe.config file located under:<InstallDirectory>\Kofax\TotalAgility\Transformation Designer\.
Update or add the following section under <system.diagnostics>:
Restart the Transformation Designer.
Replicate the issue.
Review the TransConf_tracing_output.svclog log file.
For TA Version 2025.2+:
You can enable logging by following these steps:
1 - Open the configuration file located at \Program Files (x86)\Tungsten\TotalAgility\ProjectBuilder.exe.config.
2 - In the configuration file, locate the following section:
<!-- New logSettings section -->
 <logSettings>
 <add key="EnableLogging" value="false" />
 <add key="LogLevel" value="Debug" />
 <add key="LogPath" value="C:\Temp" />
 <add key="PurgeDays" value="5" />
 </logSettings>
3 - To enable logging set the EnableLogging value to "true".
4 - Set the LogLevel value to one of the following values:
Debug (Default) - records fine-grained informational events that you can use to troubleshoot and debug the application.
Error - records error events that may allow the application to continue running.
Info - Records informational messages that highlight the progress of the application at a detailed level.
Warning - Records potentially harmful situations.
5- Set the LogPath value to a location where you want to store the logs. (Default: C:\Temp)
6 - Set the PurgeDays value to the number of days to retain the log files. (Default: 5)
7 - Save the configuration file.
Each time a project is opened, a new log file is created with the following naming format:
Tungsten_PB_YYYY_MM_DD_HH24.MI.SE-UniqueID.txt, where HH24 is the hour in 24-hour format, MI is the minute, and SE is the seconds.
For example, Tungsten_PB_2024-10-14_14.57.49-20196.txt.
8 - Restart the Transformation Designer.
9 - Replicate the issue.
10 - Review the log files generated
When to use this feature?
If you experiencing errors when loading the Transformation Designer or within the Transformation Designer, enable additional logging when replicating the behaviour, and review the output of the error log.
Notes
If the log file is not being created, ensure you run Transformation Designer as Administrator.
The log file for TA version 8+ should be located in the same directory as the Kofax.ProjectBuilder.exe.config file.
This logging method is compatible with TA version 7.4 and above, with updated steps for version 8+.

To enable the FILESTREAM functionality in SQL, go to Start -> Programs -> SQL Server Configuration Manager -> SQL Server Instance -> Properties -> FILESTREAM. The settings should look as shown below.
The filestream access level needs to be changed to allow access for SQL queries and Windows Streaming. This can be done using the below T-SQL code.
EXEC sp_configure filestream_access_level, 2 
RECONFIGURE
NOTE:
The SQL Server service will need to be restarted for this change to take affect
On the
TotalAgility_
Documents
database, a FILESTREAM filegroup and database file should be created to store the binary data on the file system. The below SQL will create:
A FILESTREAM filegroup called
DocumentBinaryData
A database file called
BinaryData
at C:\temp\FileStream that uses the
DocumentBinaryData
filegroup
USE [master]
GO
ALTER DATABASE [TotalAgility_Documents] ADD FILEGROUP [DocumentBinaryData] CONTAINS FILESTREAM 
GO
ALTER DATABASE [TotalAgility_Documents] ADD FILE (NAME=N'BinaryData', FILENAME=N'C:\temp\FileStream') TO FILEGROUP [DocumentBinaryData]
GO
After everything has been configured in SQL, the KTA Configuration Utility can be used to change the
Capture Storage
settings from SQL Server to SQL Server and File System. Save the settings.
WARNING:
After saving, this change cannot be reverted so we'd advise backing up all KTA databases prior to making this change.
After making this change, any binary data greater than 512Kb will be stored on the file system. Any binary data less than this threshold will be stored in the database. This value has been configured for optimal performance with small to medium-sized files.
Additional steps for 7.11 and above
From KTA v7.11 it necessary to specific a group for both the capture binaries and the capture project, it
cannot
be one of the other.
Note:
The value for the file size threshold is in kb, so if you want to change the value then please adjust the value accordingly if required.
If somehow you have already enabled filestream functionality during installation, then please use the ConfigUtility to verify and adjust if required.
This project needs to be defined on the TotalAgility DB. Example below:
USE [master]
GO
ALTER DATABASE [TotalAgility] ADD FILEGROUP [ProjectData] CONTAINS FILESTREAM
GO
ALTER DATABASE [TotalAgility] ADD FILE (NAME=N'ProjectData', FILENAME=N'C:\temp\ProjectFileStream') TO FILEGROUP [ProjectData]
GO

The OmniPage soft timeout can be increased through scripting in the extraction group project holding the OCR configuration - which is used during PDF generation when text is required.
The hard timeout is the soft timeout + 10 seconds.
By increasing the soft timeout the hard timeout is also increased.
The following script should be added at
Project level

Private Sub Application_InitializeScript()
 Dim profile As MpsPageRecogProfileOmniPage

OmniPage
")
 profile.Timeout =
600000
End Sub
Make sure to set the name to the name of the profile used, by default OmniPage.
The 600000 value used in the script is 10 minutes, the value should be set according to your requirements (test your images on a non-production environment to check the value required)

600000L

Release the Classification Group

Any image passing through the AI Proxy, such as Azure AI OCR or Gen AI requests might return this error. As the error says, this is caused by image dimensions, which refers to the print size of the image. A common point of confusion: the physical paper shown in the image might be a standard letter size, but the actual calculated print size of the image is what matters.
As described in
the documentation
:
The size of an image must be between the ISO A6 and A2 paper sizes. This means that images must be at least 105 mm x 148 mm (4.1 x 5.8 inches) and at most 420 mm x 594 mm (16.5 x 23.4 inches).
Handling large images
The way to handle large images is to scale them as described here:
Avoiding errors by scaling document images
Example error

Column XML TotalAgility Batch class name Process name The availability of the batch class name field in the XML overrides the setting in the import configuration. If the batch class name is not specified in the Kofax XML, then the process configured using import configuration is used. Form type name in Document tag Classification group and document type Kofax XML provides the classification group and document type in the documents FormTypeName attribute, delimited by "|". Example: FormTypeName=ClassGroup|DocType . The selection of document type is determined by the process type. If the document type is not specified in the Kofax XML, then the document type is selected from the process type. Folder Class Name in Folder tag Folder type Table field values Document table columns Tables Table fields Document field values Document fields Folder field values Folder fields Batch field values Process variables Batch Totals Process variables If the Page option is selected in the Kofax XML page tag processing level field Each page tag is converted to a page. RelativeImageFilePath Path for importing external files that are referenced in XML file. The files are imported depending on the path contained in this field: Full path: The files from this path are imported. Blank: The location of the XML file is used for importing the files. Relative path: The location of the XML file is used as the base path. An e-document type document Converted to a document. If the Document option is selected in the Kofax XML page tag processing level field Each page tag is converted to a document. Each XML document tag for a Folder initialization variable Converted to a document. If the Document option is selected in the Kofax XML page tag processing level field, a TotalAgility folder with one TotalAgility document for each XML page tag, is created. This will be in one TotalAgility job. If the Page option is selected in the Kofax XML page tag processing level field, a TotalAgility folder is created. For each XML document, one TotalAgility document with all XML page tags as pages is created. This will be in one TotalAgility job. For each XML folder, one TotalAgility job is created. Each XML document for a Document initialization variable Converted to a job. If the Document option is selected in the Kofax XML page tag processing level field, for each XML page tag, a job with one TotalAgility document is created. If the Page option is selected in the Kofax XML page tag processing level field, for each XML document, a job with a document with all XML page tags as pages is created. XML folders are ignored and the documents within the folder are processed as documents without a folder. All exceptional conditions are controlled as specified below: An XML with an empty form or document type is imported as an unclassified document. An XML with an invalid form or document type can be imported as unclassified or can be rejected. If the Batch class name is not specified in XML and a process is not specified in the import configuration, the XML file is rejected. If Document or Folder fields are not defined in TotalAgility, the XML file can be either imported and marked as rejected or rejected. If there is a mismatch in the Document or Folder field type, the XML file can be either imported and marked as rejected, or rejected. If the Folder type is not available in the XML file, the XML file can be imported as unclassified, and mark rejected or can be rejected. If a file referenced in the XML file to be imported is not available at the specified path in the XML file, the XML file is rejected. All files which are not referenced in the XML file for folder-level processing are imported as unclassified documents. All non-referenced documents are grouped in one last job. All files which are not referenced in the XML file for document-level processing are imported as unclassified documents in their own jobs.
When using an import source and mapping the XML_Data to the process initialisation variables, the XML expression must be a valid XPath. For example, in the samples above, we would use the XPath below to retrieve the values of process variables 'PONumber' and 'From'. /ImportSession/Batches/Batch/BatchFields/BatchField[@Name=' From ']/@Value /ImportSession/Batches/Batch/BatchFields/BatchField[@Name=' PONumber ']/@Value

Access issues where Kofax software cannot access certain files.

Process Monitor for Troubleshooting Kofax Issues
